title: 7 AI Trends Shaping Agentic Commerce in 2026
summary: Overview of the consumer and technology trends defining agentic commerce in 2026, including delegation of the purchase journey to AI agents within predefined boundaries.
relevance_to_sharp: Frames the shift from a human browsing a store to an agent executing the whole journey, the precondition for questioning physical/mental availability.

title: Agentic Commerce Is Redefining Retail - How to Respond
summary: BCG argues agentic commerce will reshape retail and estimates roughly $1.3 trillion of spending will be influenced, urging brands and retailers to respond now.
relevance_to_sharp: Quantifies how much purchasing moves through agents, the size of the market where the old availability rules are in question.

title: How agentic AI will reshape shopping in 2026
summary: eMarketer outlines how agentic AI changes discovery, comparison, and transaction behavior, with AI platforms projected to account for ~$20.9B in retail spending in 2026.
relevance_to_sharp: Documents the behavioral shift in how consumers discover and decide, the demand side of the availability question.

title: What Is Agentic Commerce? 45% of Shoppers Use AI (2026 Guide)
summary: Guide citing that a large share of shoppers now use AI in some part of the journey (product ideas 45%, review summaries 37%, price comparison 32%) and that ~70% are at least somewhat comfortable with an agent purchasing on their behalf.
key_quote: 73% of consumers are already using AI in their shopping journey
relevance_to_sharp: Adoption data showing humans increasingly delegating the very decisions Sharp's laws assumed they made themselves.

title: Agentic AI poised to disrupt retail, even with 50% of consumers cautious of fully autonomous purchases
summary: Bain finds agentic AI poised to redefine the customer journey while about half of shoppers remain cautious about fully autonomous purchases; 30-45% of US consumers already use GenAI for product research and comparison.
key_quote: around half of shoppers still say they are cautious about letting AI agents autonomously handle purchases from start to finish
relevance_to_sharp: Shows delegation is partial and trust-gated, defining which decisions still involve a human and which do not.

title: Agentic shopping, outcome-driven AI, and creator commerce: Takeaways from Shoptalk 2026
summary: Industry takeaways from Shoptalk 2026 on how agentic shopping and outcome-driven AI are changing the retail conversation.
relevance_to_sharp: Industry-consensus signal on how fast agent-mediated shopping is moving from concept to practice.

title: The rise of agentic shopping: Why challenger brands will win the AI era
summary: Argues AI agents do not reward incumbency the way human memory does; they weigh attribute-level data, so challenger brands with better structured data can win consideration.
key_quote: AI agents are immune to nostalgia
relevance_to_sharp: Directly challenges mental availability: if agents are immune to brand nostalgia and salience, the mental side of Sharp's model may not transfer.

title: The Era of Agentic Commerce: A Strategic Framework for the Dual-Interface Brand
summary: Proposes brands must serve a dual interface, one for humans and one for AI agents, shifting from SEO to agentic optimization and deploying brand agents that operate continuously in commerce ecosystems.
key_quote: AI agents prioritize the specific parameters of a user's prompt, requiring granular, attribute-level data rather than broad, mass-appeal marketing claims
relevance_to_sharp: Reframes distinctive assets and salience as machine-readable attributes, a candidate replacement for mental availability.

title: Agentic commerce: How AI shopping agents can change retail (the automation curve)
summary: McKinsey maps an automation curve for how much of the purchase decision consumers hand to agents by category, and what it means for brand consideration.
relevance_to_sharp: Provides a category-by-category view of where agent decisioning displaces human consideration, the gradient along which Sharp's laws weaken.

title: Agentic Commerce: Make Your Brand Unmissable
summary: Accenture argues brands must become legible and trustworthy to agents, and reports rising fraud and trust concerns: 78% of financial institutions expect a rise in AI-agent-linked fraud.
key_quote: 87% of CTOs and heads of payments at financial institutions believe that trust will be the most significant barrier to the adoption of agentic payments
relevance_to_sharp: Replaces 'be salient' with 'be machine-legible and trusted', a reformulation of availability for agents.

title: Preparing Your Brand for Agentic AI
summary: HBR guidance on how brands should adapt positioning, data, and measurement when AI agents mediate discovery and purchase.
relevance_to_sharp: A management-press articulation of how brand-building changes when the buyer is an agent.

title: Agentic commerce: Compete in an AI-buyer world
summary: PwC frames the 'AI-buyer world' and what brands and retailers must do to remain competitive when an agent is the buyer.
relevance_to_sharp: Names the core premise of the keynote, an AI buyer, and its competitive implications.

title: The agentic commerce opportunity: How AI agents are ushering in a new era for consumers and merchants
summary: McKinsey sizes the agentic commerce opportunity (as much as $1T orchestrated US retail revenue and $3-5T globally by 2030) and describes the new consumer-merchant dynamic.
key_quote: agentic commerce could generate as much as $1 trillion in orchestrated US retail revenue by 2030
relevance_to_sharp: Headline market-size case for why the rules-of-the-game question matters commercially.

title: When AI meets desire: Innovating human-centered luxury experiences in the agentic age
summary: Explores how luxury and desire-driven categories, which depend on emotion and brand aura, navigate an agentic age where agents optimize on attributes.
relevance_to_sharp: Tests the limits of the thesis: categories built on emotion and mental availability may resist agent flattening most.

title: 2030 Forecast: How Agentic AI Will Reshape US Retail
summary: Bain forecasts the US agentic commerce market at roughly $300-500B by 2030, equal to 15-25% of eCommerce, and distinguishes third-party, on-site, and off-site agents.
key_quote: third-party 'objective' agents pose the greatest threat to disintermediate retailers
relevance_to_sharp: Identifies who controls the agent surface, the new 'shelf', and who risks being disintermediated.

title: Agentic commerce (merchant guide)
summary: Shopify's merchant-facing explainer on agentic commerce and how its merchants can sell through AI agents and stay discoverable.
relevance_to_sharp: Platform view of how millions of merchants get represented to agents, the supply side of agent availability.

title: Agentic Payments in B2C Commerce: Where We Are Now
summary: Forrester's status check on agentic payments in B2C, assessing readiness across the discovery-to-checkout flow.
relevance_to_sharp: Reality check on how far agent-led transactions actually reach today versus the hype.

title: Bain & Company Forecasts Up to $500 Billion by 2030 for Agentic Commerce
summary: Secondary analysis of Bain's forecast, emphasizing that retailers must now serve both human and AI-agent customers.
key_quote: Retailers Must Now Serve Both Human and AI Agent Customers
relevance_to_sharp: Crystallizes the dual-customer reality: brands now market to humans and to agents simultaneously.

title: Buy it in ChatGPT: Instant Checkout and the Agentic Commerce Protocol
summary: OpenAI launches Instant Checkout in ChatGPT and, with Stripe, releases the Agentic Commerce Protocol (ACP) as an open standard for agent-initiated purchases.
key_quote: If a merchant already processes payments with Stripe, they can enable agentic payments in as little as one line of code
relevance_to_sharp: The new point of purchase: the buy now happens inside the agent's conversation, not on a shelf or a brand's site.

title: Supporting additional payment methods for agentic commerce
summary: Stripe describes its Agentic Commerce Suite covering discovery, checkout, payments, and fraud handling through a single integration so businesses can sell on AI agents.
relevance_to_sharp: Infrastructure that makes a brand transactable by an agent, a literal form of physical availability for machines.

title: Visa Intelligent Commerce: Enabling AI agents to buy securely and seamlessly
summary: Visa Intelligent Commerce embeds payment credentials, controls, authentication, and protections into agent-initiated transactions; Visa Intelligent Commerce Connect offers one integration supporting four agent protocols, with Akamai providing bot and behavioral intelligence under the Trusted Agent Protocol.
key_quote: Visa's product supports payments initiated through four major agent protocols: Trusted Agent Protocol, Machine Payments Protocol, Agentic Commerce Protocol and Universal Commerce Protocol
relevance_to_sharp: Establishes agent identity and trust rails, deciding which agents are allowed to transact and how brands are reached.

title: Visa and Mastercard both launch new agentic AI payments tools
summary: Coverage of Visa and Mastercard simultaneously launching agentic AI payment tools, including Mastercard Agent Pay with tokenized credentials and Payment Passkeys.
relevance_to_sharp: The card networks racing to own agent trust rails signals how central infrastructure is to agent-era availability.

title: Adyen Announces Adyen Agentic as the Universal Translator for the Next Era of Commerce
summary: Adyen launches Adyen Agentic, modular APIs (Agentic Feed, Agentic Cart, Agentic Payments) that let enterprises sell through conversational AI platforms without rebuilding commerce systems per channel; early partners include Amex, Mastercard, Salesforce, Visa, and retailers ESW, Scheels, Sezane, and SharkNinja.
key_quote: the universal translator for the next era of commerce
relevance_to_sharp: The 'Agentic Feed' layer is, in effect, how a brand's distinctive assets get translated into the structured data an agent reads.

title: Visa opens one integration for AI agent payments
summary: Reports on Visa Intelligent Commerce Connect giving merchants, agent builders, and payment enablers a single integration into agentic commerce across major protocols.
relevance_to_sharp: Consolidation toward one integration lowers the barrier for brands to become agent-transactable.

title: Agentic Commerce: When AI Buys on Your Behalf, Who Pays, Who's Liable?
summary: Examines the unresolved liability question when an agent makes an unauthorized or harmful purchase, across consumer, AI provider, merchant, and platform.
key_quote: when an AI agent makes an unauthorised or harmful purchase, who is liable, the consumer, the AI provider, the merchant or the platform?
relevance_to_sharp: Trust and liability become preconditions for delegation, gating how far the agent-buyer model can spread.

title: Agentic commerce: The next legal frontier in AI-powered shopping
summary: Legal analysis of contract formation, consent, and consumer-rights questions raised when agents transact on a consumer's behalf.
relevance_to_sharp: Clarifies whether an agent's purchase forms a binding contract, foundational to the agent-buyer premise.

title: Agentic AI Commerce: The Next Wave of Online Shopping and Retailer Risk
summary: Surveys retailer risk in agentic commerce, including manipulation, mispurchase, and the allocation of liability among consumers, merchants, and AI providers.
key_quote: protecting against AI agent manipulation (25% vulnerability rate)
relevance_to_sharp: Manipulation risk reframes 'persuasion', the soft core of mental availability, as an attack surface rather than a marketing lever.

title: Agentic Commerce Is Coming, but Regulation Meant for Humans Will Slow It Down
summary: Argues existing human-oriented rules (consent, disclosure, authentication) create friction for agent transactions and that no jurisdiction yet regulates agentic commerce specifically.
key_quote: no jurisdiction has enacted regulation specifically addressing agentic commerce
relevance_to_sharp: Shows the rulebook itself is being rewritten, the literal 'rules of the game' your keynote addresses.

title: EU AI Act: What It Means for Agentic Commerce
summary: Reviews how the EU AI Act, PSD3, GDPR, and the Consumer Rights Directive overlap on agentic commerce without clearly resolving authorization and liability.
relevance_to_sharp: European regulatory lens relevant to the keynote audience and to how agent availability will be governed.

title: Microsoft propels retail forward with agentic AI capabilities for every retail function
summary: Microsoft unveils agentic AI for retail including Copilot Checkout (buy inside Copilot) and Brand Agents for Shopify merchants, enabling conversational discovery, recommendations and outfit building.
relevance_to_sharp: Brand Agents and Copilot Checkout turn the brand into a conversational agent surface, replacing shelf presence with in-assistant presence.

title: Microsoft introduces agentic AI tools to automate retail and B2B commerce operations
summary: Copilot Checkout lets shoppers buy inside Copilot, integrating PayPal, Shopify and Stripe, with launch brands including Urban Outfitters, Anthropologie and Etsy sellers.
key_quote: Copilot Checkout ... enables shoppers to complete purchases directly within the Copilot app, eliminating the need to redirect to external websites
relevance_to_sharp: The transaction moves inside the assistant; being easy to buy now means being integrated into the agents checkout, not on a retailers site.

title: Gartner Predicts 60% of Brands Will Use Agentic AI to Deliver Streamlined One-to-One Interactions by 2028
summary: Gartner forecasts that by 2028, 60% of brands will use agentic AI to facilitate streamlined one-to-one customer interactions.
key_quote: By 2028, 60% of brands will use agentic AI to facilitate streamlined one-to-one interactions
relevance_to_sharp: If brands shift to one-to-one agent interactions, mass mental-availability reach gives way to personalised, agent-mediated engagement.

title: Gartner: AI agents will command $15 trillion in B2B purchases by 2028
summary: Gartner forecasts AI agents will intermediate more than $15 trillion in B2B spending by 2028, with autonomous buying, machine-to-machine negotiation and data-verification frameworks going mainstream.
key_quote: AI agents will intermediate more than $15 trillion in B2B spending by 2028
relevance_to_sharp: Extends the agent-buyer question to B2B, where machine-to-machine negotiation replaces relationship and brand salience entirely.

title: Agentic Commerce Impact Could Reach $385 Billion by 2030
summary: Morgan Stanley estimates agentic shoppers could represent $190-385 billion of US e-commerce by 2030 (10-20% share), with groceries and CPG the largest growth driver; about 23% of Americans bought using AI in the past month.
key_quote: Agentic shoppers could represent $190 billion to $385 billion in U.S. e-commerce spending by 2030
relevance_to_sharp: Confirms agent buying concentrates first in low-emotion replenishment categories, where mental availability mattered least.

title: ChatGPT Commerce & Agentic Shopping Statistics 2026
summary: Aggregated data showing ChatGPT drives about 20% of Walmarts and Etsys referral traffic and about 15% of Targets (Similarweb), with most agentic activity on product and search pages rather than checkout.
key_quote: ChatGPT now drives 20% of Walmart's referral traffic
relevance_to_sharp: Shows the agent becoming the discovery surface (the new shelf), redirecting where physical availability must be won.

title: Akamai AI Brand Presence
summary: Akamai product for controlling how AI systems read, interpret and present a brands content: detects AI agents at the edge, serves structured machine-readable content, and tracks brand visibility and citations across ChatGPT, Claude, Gemini and Perplexity (AI Engine Optimization).
key_quote: AI is becoming the primary entry point to your brand, shaping how users engage before they ever visit your website.
relevance_to_sharp: Reframes mental availability for the machine age: brand presence becomes structured, machine-readable content agents parse, not memory structures in a human mind.

title: Akamai AI Brand Presence - Product Brief
summary: Product brief detailing Akamai AI Brand Presence: real-time edge detection of AI agents, delivery of optimized structured content without CMS changes, and a dashboard for AI traffic, referrals and citations.
key_quote: AI systems don't browse like humans. They parse, summarize, and generate responses, which requires structured, machine-readable content.
relevance_to_sharp: Direct evidence for the thesis: winning the agent requires machine-readable structured content, the agent-era successor to distinctive brand assets.
